<!-- What does this PR do? Why is it needed? Link to related issue if applicable. -->

Closes #

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Refactor (code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature)
- [ ] Chore (dependency updates, CI changes, documentation, etc.)
- [ ] Database migration

## Screenshots / Recordings

<!-- If UI changes, add before/after screenshots or a screen recording. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] I have tested this locally and it works as expected
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with no errors (`tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] ESLint passes with no warnings (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] No `console.log` statements in source code (use logger utility)
- [ ] Types are accurate — no new `any` usage without justification
- [ ] UI changes are mobile responsive (tested at 375px, 768px, 1440px)
- [ ] API changes are backward compatible (or migration plan documented)
- [ ] Database migrations are reversible
- [ ] Environment variables are documented in `.env.example`
- [ ] I have added/updated relevant comments and documentation
- [ ] Sensitive data (keys, tokens, PII) is not hardcoded or logged
